proven.claim: PROVEN: isTrivialIdentity returning true implies asserts.length == 2
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 106
; PROVEN: isTrivialIdentity returning true implies asserts.length == 2
; Code transition: guard (asserts.length !== 2) -> return false
; Code transition: result = has_eq AND has_neg (after guard passes)

(declare-const asserts_len Int)
(declare-const has_eq Int)
(declare-const has_neg Int)
(declare-const result Int)

; Guard transition: result cannot be 1 when length != 2
(assert (=> (not (= asserts_len 2)) (= result 0)))
; Conjunction transition: result = has_eq * has_neg when guard passes
(assert (=> (= asserts_len 2) (= result (* has_eq has_neg))))
; Boolean bounds for has_eq and has_neg
(assert (>= has_eq 0))
(assert (<= has_eq 1))
(assert (>= has_neg 0))
(assert (<= has_neg 1))

; Negate the property: result=1 AND asserts_len != 2
(assert (= result 1))
(assert (not (= asserts_len 2)))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: isTrivialIdentity returns true when equality and negation assert
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 106
; VIOLATION: isTrivialIdentity returns true when equality and negation assert
; different variable pairs — (= x y) and (not (= a b)) where x != a.
; The two .some() calls are independent; no cross-check links eq_lhs to neg_lhs.

(declare-const eq_lhs Int)    ; variable-name token (encoded as ID) in equality LHS
(declare-const eq_rhs Int)    ; variable-name token in equality RHS
(declare-const neg_lhs Int)   ; variable-name token in negation LHS
(declare-const neg_rhs Int)   ; variable-name token in negation RHS
(declare-const has_eq Int)    ; 1 = hasEquality regex matched
(declare-const has_neg Int)   ; 1 = hasNegation regex matched
(declare-const fn_result Int) ; isTrivialIdentity return value (1 = true)

; Code transition: has_eq=1 because equality regex matched — eq_lhs and eq_rhs are valid IDs
(assert (= has_eq 1))
(assert (> eq_lhs 0))
(assert (> eq_rhs 0))

; Code transition: has_neg=1 because negation regex matched — neg_lhs and neg_rhs are valid IDs
(assert (= has_neg 1))
(assert (> neg_lhs 0))
(assert (> neg_rhs 0))

; Code transition: fn_result = has_eq * has_neg (guard passed: asserts.length == 2)
(assert (= fn_result (* has_eq has_neg)))

; Violation: LHS variables differ between patterns — no true contradiction exists
(assert (not (= eq_lhs neg_lhs)))

(check-sat)
